last_result: C117 complete. igt-p1b-pilot-design.md written: a concrete runnable pilot for the P1b arm of the IGT P1 forward prediction. 8 publicly-released instruction-tuned models (Llama-3.1-8B, Llama-3.2-3B, Qwen2.5-7B/14B, Mistral-7B-v0.3, Gemma-2-9B, Phi-3-medium-14B, DeepSeek-V2-Lite-Chat); 6 benchmark categories specified by task description only; 3 independent labs minimum; 18 paired Spearman ρ measurements with SE ≈ 0.089 — adequately powered for ρ < 0.50 vs. control ρ > 0.85. Cost ~$30K, calendar 6-8 weeks. Critically: requires no R1b infrastructure — only public model weights and task descriptions, both available today. The pilot bridges full P1 study: behavioral arm now, circuit arm after R1b adoption. D4 streak: 108.
next_step: C118+ options: (a) Network spine update — incorporate C116 P1 + C117 P1b pilot into network-spine-c106.md §9 and interpretability-grounding-si.md; (b) Pre-registration template — turn §7 of pilot design into a fillable OSF pre-reg form (one further step toward 'actually runnable'); (c) MCCF per-instantiation confidence — IGE-MCCF eliminates independence uncertainty for physically-orthogonal instantiations; (d) NCF checkNCF tool sketch — automated consistency checker for NCF deposits; (e) ZR passive maintenance cycle
